title: Plan 2 — Ingester domain logic (join queue + normalize + live + backfill + sweep)
description: Real ingester pipeline replacing the no-credentials skeleton. Wires Telethon NewMessage handler with channel join queue worker, post normalization, photo download to MinIO, backfill on join, catchup on restart, and ref_count sweep that leaves channels with no subscribers. End state: full event-driven ingest pipeline works end-to-end against a fake Telethon client + real Postgres + real MinIO. Plan doc at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-17-ingester-domain.md (local-only, gitignored). 5 phases: P0 session boot, P1 join worker, P2 normalize+repo, P3 photos, P4 live+backfill+catchup+sweep.

title: Plan 1 — Foundation: scaffold + DB + skeletons
description:
Implementation of Plan 1 (Foundation) from docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-17-foundation.md (local-only file).

End state: make bootstrap && make up && make test all succeed. All containers healthy. Migrations applied. /internal/health returns 200.

The plan defines 30 numbered tasks across 7 phases (see plan doc for full step-by-step TDD breakdown). The phases are tracked as separate child issues so progress is visible in bd ready/list.

Phases:
- P0 Project bootstrap (pyproject, Dockerfile, .env.example) — Tasks 1-4
- P1 Docker Compose stack (compose, override, init.sql, nginx, Makefile, bootstrap.sh) — Tasks 5-10
- P2 Backend shared module (config, logging, db, storage, redis) — Tasks 11-15
- P3 Domain models (base, User, Channel, Post, Media, user_state, admin, queue) — Tasks 16-21
- P4 Alembic + first migration + integration tests — Tasks 22-25
- P5 FastAPI skeleton with /internal/health — Task 26
- P6 Ingester skeleton with graceful shutdown — Task 27
- P7 End-to-end verification + README — Tasks 28-30

Do not implement until user dispatches via superpowers:subagent-driven-development or superpowers:executing-plans.
close_reason: Plan 1 — Foundation: all 7 phases complete; 20/20 tests; E2E up and down

title: Telegram Feed MVP — TMA + ingester + admin
description:
Multi-user Telegram Mini App for aggregated channel feed with backend service for ingest/normalization/storage.

Approved design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-17-telegram-feed-design.md (local-only, not in repo)

Stack:
- Backend: Python 3.12 + FastAPI + Telethon + SQLAlchemy + Alembic
- Frontend (TMA): React 18 + Vite + @telegram-apps/sdk-react + TanStack Query
- Sysadmin: separate React SPA with login + TOTP
- Infra: PostgreSQL 16, MinIO, Redis 7, nginx — all via Docker Compose
- Hosting: VPS for prod, identical local dev stack via docker-compose.override.yml
- Source of posts: MTProto userbot via Telethon (NewMessage events + backfill)

Architecture: split ingester worker + FastAPI; they communicate via PostgreSQL only.

MVP feature scope (per business reqs):
- Telegram auth via initData
- Scrollable feed of posts from connected sources (pull-to-refresh delivery)
- Save / hide post / hide source
- User-managed source list
- Sysadmin panel for system-wide channel management
- Backend ingest, normalization, storage of posts
- Photo media in MinIO, videos via tg:// open-in-Telegram

Out of MVP: search, push, read/unread, video streaming proxy, sharding, archival.

title: Ingester P4 — Live ingest + backfill + catchup + ref_count sweep + e2e
description: Plan 2 Phase 4 (Tasks 7-10). ingester/live.py with on_new_message handler and subscribe_to_active_channels. Backfill integrated into join_worker (iter_messages last 50). catchup_channels at startup before subscribing live. ingester/refcount_sweep.py LeaveChannelRequest when ref_count=0. Integration test with FakeTelethonClient against testcontainers PG + MinIO. Wires final main.py with asyncio.gather of 3 tasks.

title: Ingester P3 — Photo download to MinIO
description: Plan 2 Phase 3 (Task 6). ingester/photos.py: download_and_store(client, minio, msg) for photos and download_video_thumbnail() for videos. Bytes via client.download_media(msg.photo, bytes), put_object to MinIO with key photos/{channel_id}/{msg_id}_{photo_id}.jpg. AsyncMock-based unit tests.

title: Ingester P2 — Post normalization (pure) + posts repository
description: Plan 2 Phase 2 (Task 5). ingester/normalize.py: pure (Message, channel_id) -> (post_dict, media_dicts) covering text/html/photo/video/document/album. repositories/posts.py: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING idempotent upsert. storage_key left None — actual media download is P3. Unit + integration tests.

title: Ingester P1 — Channel join queue worker + ref_count repositories
description: Plan 2 Phase 1 (Tasks 3-4). Create repositories/channels.py + repositories/join_queue.py with upsert/inc/dec/pop/mark functions. Implement ingester/join_worker.py polling channel_join_queue every 2s, calling get_entity + JoinChannelRequest, handling FloodWait/UsernameNotOccupied/ChannelPrivate. Wire into main.py via asyncio.gather. Backfill NOT in this phase.

title: Ingester P0 — Real Telethon session boot (client_factory + session)
description: Plan 2 Phase 0 (Tasks 1-2). Create shared/tg/client_factory.py and ingester/session.py. Refactor ingester/main.py to use make_client. Update existing test_ingester_main.py to patch the new factory. Phase ends with ingester actually connecting to Telegram (when creds set) — still no event handlers yet.

title: bootstrap.sh portability: python3 resolves to Windows Store stub
description: infra/scripts/bootstrap.sh uses python3 - <<PY which on Windows resolves to the Microsoft Store reparse-point stub (exit 49) instead of any real Python. Replace with py -3.12 or with sed/awk for portability. Reproduced during P7 verification; manual workaround used to unblock.

title: Foundation P7 — End-to-end verification + README
description: Plan 1 Phase 7 (Tasks 28-30). make bootstrap → make up → migrate → curl /internal/health → make test full pass. README.md with quickstart.

comments.author: supperik
comments.text: Done. Single commit a89ce0c. E2E verified: bootstrap → build → migrate → curl health 200 → 13 tables in PG → ingester no_credentials warn → docker compose down clean. Full suite 20/20. Side-quests filed as telegram-feed-2ls (bootstrap.sh python3 on Windows) and telegram-feed-5wm (health version 0.0.0 due to Dockerfile --no-root). Phase 4 config bug fixed inline (tg_api_id empty string → field_validator coerces to 0) since it blocked api boot.

title: Foundation P2 — Backend shared module (config, logging, db, storage, redis)
description: Plan 1 Phase 2 (Tasks 11-15). TDD-driven: pydantic Settings, structlog config, async SQLAlchemy engine, MinIO client, Redis async client. Each with a unit test.
close_reason: P2 complete, spec corrected for structlog/PrintLoggerFactory compat

comments.author: supperik
comments.text: Done with concerns. 5 commits 7d9f400..90717c2, all 6 unit tests pass. Real spec bug found: structlog.stdlib.add_logger_name is incompatible with PrintLoggerFactory (PrintLogger has no .name attr → AttributeError on every log call). Subagent removed add_logger_name from shared_processors. Concern: .gitkeep files remain in src/api/ and src/ingester/ (subagent honored conservative scope rule — they get removed in P5/P6 naturally).

description: Plan 1 Phase 1 (Tasks 5-10). docker-compose.yml + override, infra/postgres/init.sql, infra/nginx/nginx.conf, Makefile, infra/scripts/bootstrap.sh. Verifies: docker compose config parses cleanly.

comments.author: supperik
comments.text: Done by subagent. Commits fca6f61..d815ec8 (6 total). 5 concerns flagged, all benign: docker daemon was off and was started; nginx -t needed --add-host upstream stub; entrypoint bypass for nginx image; exec bit via git update-index on Windows; beads hook noise. All verifications passed.

title: Foundation P0 — Project bootstrap (pyproject, Dockerfile, .env.example)
description: Plan 1 Phase 0 (Tasks 1-4). Directory skeleton, backend/pyproject.toml with full stack deps, backend/Dockerfile, .env.example. See docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-17-foundation.md

comments.author: supperik
comments.text: Done. 4 commits on plan-1-foundation: 7f82051, 80b9a97, fa5086b, 2be3ab8. Deviation: Dockerfile uses POETRY_VERSION=2.4.1 (plan said 1.8.4) because host Poetry resolved to 2.x and 1.x cannot read 2.x lock format. Poetry installed via 'py -3.12 -m pip install --user poetry'. Lock + install verified end-to-end with 138 deps in py-3.12 venv.

title: API health endpoint reports version 0.0.0 due to Dockerfile --no-root
description: backend/src/api/main.py reads version via importlib.metadata.version('telegram-feed-backend'). Dockerfile installs deps with --no-root, so the project package metadata is absent at runtime and the fallback '0.0.0' fires. Either drop --no-root from Dockerfile or read version from pyproject.toml / an embedded constant. Endpoint still returns 200; this is cosmetic.

value: Windows + Docker Desktop quirks: testcontainers Ryuk reaper flaky on 2nd boot per session (TESTCONTAINERS_RYUK_DISABLED=true workaround in backend/tests/conftest.py). bootstrap.sh uses python3 which resolves to Windows Store stub (telegram-feed-2ls). Always test cross-platform shell commands.

Poetry on this host is 2.4.1 installed via 'py -3.12 -m pip install --user poetry'; poetry is NOT on PATH. Use 'py -3.12 -m poetry run <cmd>' everywhere. Dockerfile pins POETRY_VERSION=2.4.1 (matching host lock file format, NOT the original plan's 1.8.4).
One commit = one closed beads task. Don't commit per TDD cycle or per file. Auto-commit allowed at bd close. Reasoning: per-cycle commits clutter history and hurt code review of a coherent change.

Never include Co-Authored-By Claude, 🤖 Generated with Claude Code, Generated-By, Assisted-By in commits or PRs. Settings already configured (attribution.commit=empty, includeCoAuthoredBy=false) but the Bash tool's default HEREDOC template still tries to add it — write commit messages without that trailer.

Use EnterWorktree (deferred Claude Code native tool, requires ToolSearch select:EnterWorktree first) for worktrees. NEVER run 'git worktree add' directly — creates phantom worktree invisible to the harness in .worktrees/ instead of .claude/worktrees/.
